human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for franchise and multi-unit support boundary discipline, source quality, franchise record handling, capacity recommendation logic, exception escalation, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to franchise and multi-unit support intake, evidence review, stakeholder communication, allocation recommendations, capacity-risk escalation, and handoff packaging.

Authority sources cover federal Franchise Rule text, FTC franchise compliance guidance, IFA franchise operations and scale references, ISO 10002 complaint workflow, and COSO control evidence expectations.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope allocation, missing facts, policy exceptions, handoffs, demand-over-capacity, approval-bound tradeoffs, repeat failures, legal disclosure rejection, franchise terminology, missing safety facts, conflicting policies, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/franchise-systems-and-multi-unit-enterprise-support/multi-unit-support-specialist/ so support work stays separate from legal, compliance, field operations, training, finance, real estate, safety, quality, and executive approval ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around FDD and agreement interpretation, brand-standard waivers, support-capacity approval, repeat failures, safety-sensitive requests, and conflicting-policy handling.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated franchise and multi-unit support specialist that provides evidence-backed support disposition, allocation recommendation, communication, and escalation packaging.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained-implementation runtime exists.
